description: Young/early-career photos of celebrities hosted on Wikimedia Commons. All URLs resolved via the Commons MediaWiki imageinfo API and confirmed to be hotlinkable upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/... URLs. Every file is public domain or CC-licensed.

notes: Found 46 verified young-photo entries (tiers: 10 at 200 / 21 at 400 / 15 at 600). URLs resolved directly via Commons MediaWiki imageinfo API so they are guaranteed to be real Commons-hosted files, all public domain or CC/CC0 licensed. Celebrities SKIPPED due to no suitable young photo on Commons: Madonna (only album-cover art and religious 'Madonna' paintings turn up), David Beckham (no pre-fame Commons photos), Cristiano Ronaldo (only statues/museum shots of his youth, no photos), Justin Timberlake (only later-era NSYNC / solo photos, no teenage Mickey Mouse Club), Macaulay Culkin (no Home Alone-era photos on Commons — copyright-locked), Leonardo DiCaprio (no Growing Pains/Titanic-era Commons photos), Brad Pitt (no pre-2000 Commons photos), Jennifer Aniston (no Commons photos of her young), Julia Roberts (only a 1990 image with unusual composition, borderline), Whitney Houston (only 1991 Welcome Home Heroes — close to adult peak, borderline), Rihanna (no pre-2011 photos), Drake (no Degrassi / pre-2010 photos), Kanye West (only 2005 tour photos at age 28, not early enough), Daniel Radcliffe (no young-era photos came up reliably in Commons search). For celebrities whose only young Commons photo is already close to their famous adult appearance (e.g. Tom Cruise 1989, JFK 1947, Diana 1985, Bill Clinton 1978, Donald Trump 1980s), tier is set to 400 rather than 200 because the visual gap from adult is modest. Tier 600 is reserved for celebs whose young photo requires era-specific fandom to ID (Sinatra 1944, Hendrix 1967, Dylan 1963, Keith Richards 1965, etc.). Every entry was verified by direct Commons API imageinfo lookup of the file title, so the upload.wikimedia.org URL reflects the actual current file-content hash path.
